skill.name: star-story-extraction
skill.description: Extracts interview-ready STAR stories from completed technical work, with prompts for context, ownership, actions, results, and impact.
skill.summary: Turn completed work into structured STAR interview stories.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown templates and interview coaching text. No executable code, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-provided instructional content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and a sample output path. They do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown syntax and a literal file path example. No shell, Ruby, or command execution construct is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary words and interview guidance, such as description text and prompts about feature implementation. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose, headings, and checklist text only. There is no code path, cipher use, or security-sensitive implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Interview Prompts
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts occur in STAR method questions and coaching prompts. They ask about a user's project context, not the host system or environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is career writing guidance and does not request host metadata, accounts, network details, or environment inspection.

content.value_statement: Completed technical work is often hard to explain in interviews. This skill turns project details into clear STAR stories with ownership, technical depth, and measurable impact.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through Situation, Task, Action, and Result sections.
Classifies completed work by story type, such as feature, refactor, or security fix.
Prompts for technical decisions, trade-offs, ownership, and measurable outcomes.
Provides a reusable Markdown story template for interview preparation.
Suggests interview question variations that each story can answer.
Identifies common story weaknesses, including vague impact and excessive length.
limitations: It does not verify the accuracy of claimed metrics or business impact.
It depends on the user providing enough project context and technical detail.
It does not tailor stories to a specific employer unless that context is supplied.
It is guidance content only and does not include automated tooling.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Behavioral Interviews
use_cases.description: Convert recent project work into concise answers for ownership, challenge, and impact questions.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers preparing for job interviews

use_cases.title: Coach Junior Developers
use_cases.description: Use structured questions to help a developer explain technical decisions and personal contribution.

use_cases.title: Document Career Achievements
use_cases.description: Save completed work as reusable career stories for reviews, portfolios, and resume updates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic STAR Story
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for one completed task
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me turn this completed task into a STAR interview story: [describe the task, context, my role, actions, and result].

prompt_templates.title: Find the Strongest Story Angle
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for choosing the best interview framing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project summary and identify the strongest STAR story type. Then ask me the missing questions needed to complete it.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Technical Depth
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for making an existing answer more specific
prompt_templates.prompt: Refine this STAR story for a software engineering interview. Add stronger technical decisions, trade-offs, and measurable impact where supported.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Interview Story Bank
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these completed projects and create a prioritized interview story bank. Label each story by question type and key technical points.

output_examples.input: A developer fixed a slow signup flow and wants an interview answer.
output_examples.output: A concise STAR story that explains the signup problem, the developer's ownership, the technical changes, and the measured conversion improvement.

output_examples.input: A mentor wants to help a junior engineer explain a debugging project.
output: A list of focused questions about the hardest bug, diagnostic steps, decisions made, and final impact.
A suggested story type and interview questions the story can answer.

output_examples.input: A developer has several recent accomplishments but no interview story bank.
output_examples.output: A set of organized story candidates grouped by technical challenge, feature delivery, refactoring, and learning curve.

best_practices: Provide specific project context, your personal role, and the measurable result when possible.
Use first-person language to show ownership without overstating team contributions.
Keep each final story short enough to speak in two to three minutes.
anti_patterns: Do not invent metrics or business impact that you cannot support.
Do not describe only team actions when the interviewer asked about your work.
Do not turn the story into a long technical walkthrough without a clear result.

faq.question: What is the STAR method?
faq.answer: STAR means Situation, Task, Action, and Result. It is a structure for clear interview answers.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete interview answer?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft a complete story when you provide enough context, actions, and outcome details.

faq.question: Does it work for non-engineering roles?
faq.answer: It is written for technical work, but the STAR structure can support many professional stories.

faq.question: Will it create metrics for me?
faq.answer: No. It can help phrase metrics, but you should only include results that are accurate.

faq.question: Can it help when I do not know the result?
faq.answer: Yes. It can ask questions that identify available impact signals, such as speed, quality, adoption, or learning.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is plain Markdown guidance for Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows.
